goal: status-docs-followup: review docs/status todo docs (excluding the release-todo closeout already handled), identify what is truly left, reconcile stale entries against the current codebase, and start implementation on one concrete open item.
summary: Audit /status todo docs, sync stale entries, and start the first open UX item.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/status/architecture-todo.md and docs/status/ui-todo.md reflect true open items (stale done items corrected with file evidence)
A prioritized 'what is left' inventory is captured in status docs without contradicting release-todo
At least one concrete open non-GA UI todo item is implemented with tests and status-doc hygiene updates

tasks.name: audit-status-doc-open-items
tasks.scopedGoal: Reconcile docs/status/architecture-todo.md and docs/status/ui-todo.md with the current repository so open items are accurate and stale resolved claims are corrected.
tasks.brief: Audit only docs/status backlog docs and directly related code references they cite. Update architecture-todo/ui-todo wording where items are stale (e.g., TODO references already resolved) and tighten 'what remains' text to concrete unresolved work. Do not reopen release-todo non-GA closures. Keep links to release-todo for GA §1 gating. If an item is now resolved, mark it as complete in-place with concise evidence; if still open, keep it open with a clear next action.
acceptance: architecture-todo and ui-todo open-item text no longer points at already-resolved TODOs
Open work is explicitly identified and grouped as immediate vs later workstreams
Status docs remain consistent with release-todo's current GA-only blocker posture
tasks.verify: Run targeted checks for any touched code/docs assertions (e.g., rg checks for TODO lines, related unit tests if code changed) and include evidence in handoff.

tasks.name: verify-audit-status-doc-open-items
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify that status-doc open-item inventory is accurate and free of stale resolved claims.
tasks.brief: Re-audit docs/status/architecture-todo.md and docs/status/ui-todo.md against referenced code paths. Fail if an item is still mislabeled open/resolved, if contradictory statements remain, or if release-todo linkage is inconsistent.
acceptance: Verifier confirms no obvious stale TODO references remain in architecture/ui status docs
Verifier confirms remaining work is clearly identified and actionable

tasks.name: start-auth-register-password-strength-and-tos
tasks.scopedGoal: Start implementation by closing one concrete open UI todo item: add register-form password-strength feedback and a terms-of-service link, with tests and status-doc updates.
tasks.brief: Implement the open auth-item follow-through tracked in ui-todo item 11 / Phase 2.7: password-strength indicator on register and a terms-of-service link in auth flow copy/UI. Keep implementation i18n-safe (`en` + `zh-CN`), accessible, and compatible with existing validation. Add/update focused tests. Because this closes a tracked ui-todo item subset, update docs/status/ui-todo.md and docs/status/product-done.md per AGENTS.md backlog hygiene.
acceptance: Register form surfaces password-strength feedback and a terms-of-service link accessibly
Relevant tests pass for the updated auth UX
ui-todo/product-done reflect closure progress for this started item
tasks.verify: Run focused frontend tests for modified auth components plus type/lint checks as needed for touched files.

tasks.name: verify-auth-register-password-strength-and-tos
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the first started open UI item is implemented correctly and status docs were updated truthfully.
tasks.brief: Check UX behavior, i18n coverage, and tests around register password-strength and terms link. Validate status-doc updates for ui-todo/product-done follow AGENTS.md and do not over-claim closure. Use repo-level code/test evidence only (no GUI/manual recording); treat this as a terminal-driven verification pass to avoid non-essential tool stalls.
acceptance: Verifier confirms behavior/test evidence for password-strength + terms link
Verifier confirms status docs are truthful and aligned with the partial closure scope
